model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.name: performance__agg_yearly_county
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.description:
yearly aggregation of volume,speed, and occupancy along with delays and lost productivity by
across county. This metrics will measure the yearly performance
by county. This can be used to understand the SHS performance
in perspective of spatial variation.

COUNTY.name: COUNTY
COUNTY.description: The unique number that identifies the county that contains a specific VDS within PeMS.

YEARLY_SPEED_AVG.name: YEARLY_SPEED_AVG
YEARLY_SPEED_AVG.description: flow weighted speed over the sample year.

YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM.name: YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM
YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM.description: The sum of the flow values over the sample period across county.

YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG.name: YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG
YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG.description: The average of the occupancy values over the sample period across county.

YEARLY_VMT.name: YEARLY_VMT
YEARLY_VMT.description:
The sum of the miles of freeway driven by each vehicle in a given year and
a given county.

YEARLY_VHT.name: YEARLY_VHT
YEARLY_VHT.description:
Vehicle Hours Travelled (VHT) is calculated in a given a year and a
given county. VHT is the amount of time spent by all of the
vehicles on the freeway in a given year.

YEARLY_TTI.name: YEARLY_TTI
YEARLY_TTI.description:
The Travel Time Index (TTI) is the ratio of the average travel time for all users
across a region to the free-flow travel time across county.
The free-flow travel time is taken to be the time to traverse the link when traveling at 60MPH.
For loop-based performance measures, the TTI is simply the free-flow speed divided by the
performance measure Q.

YEARLY_Q_VALUE.name: YEARLY_Q_VALUE
YEARLY_Q_VALUE.description:
Q is the sum of the VMT in a spatial and temporal region divided by the sum of
the VHT in the same region in a given year across county.
For a single location its interpretation is the average speed.

DELAY.name: DELAY
DELAY.description:
yearly delay for different threshold of congestion speed such as 35, 40, 45, 50, 55 and 60 mph
across county.
The Delay performance metric is the amount of additional time spent by the
vehicles on a section of road due to congestion.

LOST_PRODUCTIVITY.name: LOST_PRODUCTIVITY
LOST_PRODUCTIVITY.description:
This is the monthly lost productivity that is simply sum up all days
lost productivity in a given month of the year timeperiod across county.
The lost Productivity perfromance metric is
the number of lane-mile-month on the freeway lost due to operating under congested
conditions instead of under free-flow conditions.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.compiled_code:
-- read the volume, occupancy and speed daily level data
with station_daily_data as (
    select
        *,
        year(sample_date) as sample_year
    from ANALYTICS_PRD.performance.int_performance__station_metrics_agg_daily
),

-- now aggregate daily volume, occupancy and speed to weekly
spatial_metrics as (
    select
        county,
        sample_year,
        sum(daily_volume) as yearly_volume_sum,
        avg(daily_occupancy) as yearly_occupancy_avg,
        sum(daily_volume * daily_speed) / nullifzero(sum(daily_volume)) as yearly_speed_avg,
        sum(daily_vmt) as yearly_vmt,
        sum(daily_vht) as yearly_vht,
        yearly_vmt / nullifzero(yearly_vht) as yearly_q_value,
        -- travel time
        60 / nullifzero(yearly_q_value) as yearly_tti,
        
            sum(delay_35_mph)
                as delay_35_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(delay_40_mph)
                as delay_40_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(delay_45_mph)
                as delay_45_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(delay_50_mph)
                as delay_50_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(delay_55_mph)
                as delay_55_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(delay_60_mph)
                as delay_60_mph
            

        ,
        
            sum(lost_productivity_35_mph)
                as lost_productivity_35_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(lost_productivity_40_mph)
                as lost_productivity_40_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(lost_productivity_45_mph)
                as lost_productivity_45_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(lost_productivity_50_mph)
                as lost_productivity_50_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(lost_productivity_55_mph)
                as lost_productivity_55_mph
            
                ,
            

        
            sum(lost_productivity_60_mph)
                as lost_productivity_60_mph
            

        
    from station_daily_data
    group by
        county, sample_year
),

spatial_metricsc as (
    
    with county as (
        select
            county_id,
            lower(county_name) as county
        from ANALYTICS_PRD.clearinghouse.counties
    ),
    station_with_county as (
        select
            spatial_metrics.* exclude (county),
            c.county
        from spatial_metrics
        inner join county as c
        on spatial_metrics.county = c.county_id
    )

    select * from station_with_county

)

select * from spatial_metricsc

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.name: performance__station_metric_agg_recent_one_week
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.description:
Hourly aggregation of volume, vmt, and vht for HOV and mainline pairs for recent one week.
This model calculated HOV volume, vmt and vht penetration using corresponding
mainline station. The corresponding mainline station of HOV station was
determined based on proximity, and direction.

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.description: Two character string identify the VDS type.

DISTRICT.name: DISTRICT
DISTRICT.description: The district in which the VDS is located. Values are 1-12.

CITY.name: CITY
CITY.description: The city number where the VDS is located, if available.

COUNTY.name: COUNTY
COUNTY.description: The unique number that identifies the county that contains a specific VDS within PeMS.

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION
DIRECTION.description: A string indicating the freeway direction of a specific VDS. Directions are N, E, S or W.

FREEWAY.name: FREEWAY
FREEWAY.description: The freeway where the VDS is located.

HOURLY_CATEGORY.name: HOURLY_CATEGORY
HOURLY_CATEGORY.description: 24-hrs have been catagorized as am peak (6-10 am), am off peak (10 am to 3 pm), pm peak (3 pm to 7 pm) and pm off peak (7 pm to  6am).

HOV_STATION_ID.name: HOV_STATION_ID
HOV_STATION_ID.description: An integer value that uniquely indentifies of a HOV station.

HOUR_DAY.name: HOUR_DAY
HOUR_DAY.description: Hour of day varies from 0 to 23.

SAMPLE_DATE.name: SAMPLE_DATE
SAMPLE_DATE.description: The sample collection date.

SAMPLE_HOUR.name: SAMPLE_HOUR
SAMPLE_HOUR.description: The sample collection hour with date.

WEEKDAY.name: WEEKDAY
WEEKDAY.description: The day of week when the data was collected.

WEEKDAY_STATUS.name: WEEKDAY_STATUS
WEEKDAY_STATUS.description: Identify weather the data was collected in weekday or weekend.

HOV_VHT_PENETRATION.name: HOV_VHT_PENETRATION
HOV_VHT_PENETRATION.description: The ratio of HOV houlry average vht to mainline houlry average vht. it is expressed as percentage

HOV_VMT_PENETRATION.name: HOV_VMT_PENETRATION
HOV_VMT_PENETRATION.description: The ratio of HOV houlry average vmt to mainline houlry average vmt. it is expressed as percentage.

HOV_VOLUME_PENETRATION.name: HOV_VOLUME_PENETRATION
HOV_VOLUME_PENETRATION.description: The ratio of HOV houlry average volume to mainline houlry average volume. it is expressed as percentage.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.raw_code:
with station_meta as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_vds__active_stations') }}
    where active_date >= dateadd('day', -8, current_date())
),

station_county as (
    {{ get_county_name('station_meta') }}
),

station_pairs as (
    select
        ml.station_id as ml_station_id,
        ml.district,
        ml.city,
        ml.county,
        ml.freeway,
        ml.direction,
        ml.absolute_postmile as ml_absolute_postmile,
        ml.physical_lanes as ml_lanes,
        ml.active_date,
        hov.station_id as hov_station_id,
        hov.name as hov_name,
        hov.latitude as hov_latitude,
        hov.longitude as hov_longitude,
        hov.absolute_postmile as hov_absolute_postmile,
        hov.length as hov_length,
        hov.physical_lanes as hov_lanes,
        abs(ml.absolute_postmile - hov.absolute_postmile) as delta_postmile
    from
        station_county as ml
    inner join
        station_county as hov
        on
            ml.freeway = hov.freeway
            and ml.direction = hov.direction
            and ml.active_date = hov.active_date
            and ml.station_id != hov.station_id
            and ml.station_type = 'ML'
            and hov.station_type = 'HV'
            and ml.district != 4
            and hov.district != 4
),

closest_station_with_selection as (
    select
        *,
        row_number() over (partition by ml_station_id, active_date order by delta_postmile asc) as distance_ranking
    from station_pairs
    where
        delta_postmile <= 5
    qualify
        distance_ranking = 1
),

hourly_station_volume as (
    select
        station_id,
        direction,
        sample_date,
        sample_hour,
        hourly_volume,
        hourly_vmt,
        hourly_vht,
        station_type
    from {{ ref('int_performance__station_metrics_agg_hourly') }}
    where sample_date >= dateadd('day', -8, current_date())
),

/*
 * Join station metadata pairs with hourly observations. This could be
 * expressed more naturally with 2 joins, 1 for ML stations and 1 for HOV,
 * but doing so incurs a significant performance cost. Instead, we have a
 * more permissive join and an aggregation.
 */
station_with_ml_hov_metrics as (
    select
        css.*,
        hourly.sample_date,
        hourly.sample_hour,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'ML' then hourly.hourly_volume end) as ml_hourly_volume,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'ML' then hourly.hourly_vmt end) as ml_hourly_vmt,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'ML' then hourly.hourly_vht end) as ml_hourly_vht,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'HV' then hourly.hourly_volume end) as hov_hourly_volume,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'HV' then hourly.hourly_vmt end) as hov_hourly_vmt,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'HV' then hourly.hourly_vht end) as hov_hourly_vht
    from closest_station_with_selection as css
    inner join hourly_station_volume as hourly
        on
            (css.ml_station_id = hourly.station_id or css.hov_station_id = hourly.station_id)
            and css.direction = hourly.direction
            and css.active_date = hourly.sample_date
    group by all
),

station_metric_agg as (
    select
        hov_station_id,
        hov_name,
        hov_latitude,
        hov_longitude,
        avg(hov_length) as hov_length_avg,
        max(hov_absolute_postmile) as hov_absolute_postmile,
        district,
        city,
        county,
        freeway,
        direction,
        sample_date,
        sample_hour,
        (sum(ml_hourly_volume) / nullif(sum(ml_lanes), 0)) as ml_hourly_average_volume,
        (sum(ml_hourly_vmt) / nullif(sum(ml_lanes), 0)) as ml_hourly_average_vmt,
        (sum(ml_hourly_vht) / nullif(sum(ml_lanes), 0)) as ml_hourly_average_vht,
        (sum(hov_hourly_volume) / nullif(sum(hov_lanes), 0)) as hov_hourly_average_volume,
        (sum(hov_hourly_vmt) / nullif(sum(hov_lanes), 0)) as hov_hourly_average_vmt,
        (sum(hov_hourly_vht) / nullif(sum(hov_lanes), 0)) as hov_hourly_average_vht,
        (sum(hov_hourly_volume) / nullif(sum(ml_hourly_volume), 0)) * 100 as hov_volume_penetration,
        (sum(hov_hourly_vmt) / nullif(sum(ml_hourly_vmt), 0)) * 100 as hov_vmt_penetration,
        (sum(hov_hourly_vht) / nullif(sum(ml_hourly_vht), 0)) * 100 as hov_vht_penetration
    from station_with_ml_hov_metrics
    group by
        hov_station_id,
        hov_name,
        hov_latitude,
        hov_longitude,
        district,
        city,
        county,
        freeway,
        direction,
        sample_date,
        sample_hour
),

final_data_with_category as (
    select
        station_metric_agg.*,

        -- Hourly Category (Based on peak/off-peak periods)
        case
            -- AM Peak
            when
                to_time(station_metric_agg.sample_hour) between to_time({{ var("am_peak_start") }}) and to_time(
                    {{ var("am_peak_end") }}
                )
                then 'am_peak'

            -- Day Off-Peak
            when
                to_time(station_metric_agg.sample_hour) between to_time({{ var("day_off_peak_start") }}) and to_time(
                    {{ var("day_off_peak_end") }}
                )
                then 'day_off_peak'

            -- PM Peak
            when
                to_time(station_metric_agg.sample_hour) between to_time({{ var("pm_peak_start") }}) and to_time(
                    {{ var("pm_peak_end") }}
                )
                then 'pm_peak'

            -- Night Off-Peak (default)
            else 'night_off_peak'
        end as hourly_category,

        -- Weekday/Weekend Category
        case
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) in (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) then 'Weekday'
            else 'Weekend'
        end as weekday_status,

        -- Day of the Week Name
        case
            when station_metric_agg.sample_date is null then 'No Date'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 0 then 'Sunday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 1 then 'Monday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 2 then 'Tuesday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 3 then 'Wednesday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 4 then 'Thursday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 5 then 'Friday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 6 then 'Saturday'
            else 'Unknown' -- This should not normally happen
        end as weekday
    from station_metric_agg
)

select
    *,
    extract(hour from sample_hour) as hour_day
from final_data_with_category

with station_meta as (
    select * from ANALYTICS_PRD.vds.int_vds__active_stations
    where active_date >= dateadd('day', -8, current_date())
),

station_county as (
    
    with county as (
        select
            county_id,
            lower(county_name) as county
        from ANALYTICS_PRD.clearinghouse.counties
    ),
    station_with_county as (
        select
            station_meta.* exclude (county),
            c.county
        from station_meta
        inner join county as c
        on station_meta.county = c.county_id
    )

    select * from station_with_county

),

station_pairs as (
    select
        ml.station_id as ml_station_id,
        ml.district,
        ml.city,
        ml.county,
        ml.freeway,
        ml.direction,
        ml.absolute_postmile as ml_absolute_postmile,
        ml.physical_lanes as ml_lanes,
        ml.active_date,
        hov.station_id as hov_station_id,
        hov.name as hov_name,
        hov.latitude as hov_latitude,
        hov.longitude as hov_longitude,
        hov.absolute_postmile as hov_absolute_postmile,
        hov.length as hov_length,
        hov.physical_lanes as hov_lanes,
        abs(ml.absolute_postmile - hov.absolute_postmile) as delta_postmile
    from
        station_county as ml
    inner join
        station_county as hov
        on
            ml.freeway = hov.freeway
            and ml.direction = hov.direction
            and ml.active_date = hov.active_date
            and ml.station_id != hov.station_id
            and ml.station_type = 'ML'
            and hov.station_type = 'HV'
            and ml.district != 4
            and hov.district != 4
),

closest_station_with_selection as (
    select
        *,
        row_number() over (partition by ml_station_id, active_date order by delta_postmile asc) as distance_ranking
    from station_pairs
    where
        delta_postmile <= 5
    qualify
        distance_ranking = 1
),

hourly_station_volume as (
    select
        station_id,
        direction,
        sample_date,
        sample_hour,
        hourly_volume,
        hourly_vmt,
        hourly_vht,
        station_type
    from ANALYTICS_PRD.performance.int_performance__station_metrics_agg_hourly
    where sample_date >= dateadd('day', -8, current_date())
),

/*
 * Join station metadata pairs with hourly observations. This could be
 * expressed more naturally with 2 joins, 1 for ML stations and 1 for HOV,
 * but doing so incurs a significant performance cost. Instead, we have a
 * more permissive join and an aggregation.
 */
station_with_ml_hov_metrics as (
    select
        css.*,
        hourly.sample_date,
        hourly.sample_hour,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'ML' then hourly.hourly_volume end) as ml_hourly_volume,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'ML' then hourly.hourly_vmt end) as ml_hourly_vmt,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'ML' then hourly.hourly_vht end) as ml_hourly_vht,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'HV' then hourly.hourly_volume end) as hov_hourly_volume,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'HV' then hourly.hourly_vmt end) as hov_hourly_vmt,
        max(case when hourly.station_type = 'HV' then hourly.hourly_vht end) as hov_hourly_vht
    from closest_station_with_selection as css
    inner join hourly_station_volume as hourly
        on
            (css.ml_station_id = hourly.station_id or css.hov_station_id = hourly.station_id)
            and css.direction = hourly.direction
            and css.active_date = hourly.sample_date
    group by all
),

station_metric_agg as (
    select
        hov_station_id,
        hov_name,
        hov_latitude,
        hov_longitude,
        avg(hov_length) as hov_length_avg,
        max(hov_absolute_postmile) as hov_absolute_postmile,
        district,
        city,
        county,
        freeway,
        direction,
        sample_date,
        sample_hour,
        (sum(ml_hourly_volume) / nullif(sum(ml_lanes), 0)) as ml_hourly_average_volume,
        (sum(ml_hourly_vmt) / nullif(sum(ml_lanes), 0)) as ml_hourly_average_vmt,
        (sum(ml_hourly_vht) / nullif(sum(ml_lanes), 0)) as ml_hourly_average_vht,
        (sum(hov_hourly_volume) / nullif(sum(hov_lanes), 0)) as hov_hourly_average_volume,
        (sum(hov_hourly_vmt) / nullif(sum(hov_lanes), 0)) as hov_hourly_average_vmt,
        (sum(hov_hourly_vht) / nullif(sum(hov_lanes), 0)) as hov_hourly_average_vht,
        (sum(hov_hourly_volume) / nullif(sum(ml_hourly_volume), 0)) * 100 as hov_volume_penetration,
        (sum(hov_hourly_vmt) / nullif(sum(ml_hourly_vmt), 0)) * 100 as hov_vmt_penetration,
        (sum(hov_hourly_vht) / nullif(sum(ml_hourly_vht), 0)) * 100 as hov_vht_penetration
    from station_with_ml_hov_metrics
    group by
        hov_station_id,
        hov_name,
        hov_latitude,
        hov_longitude,
        district,
        city,
        county,
        freeway,
        direction,
        sample_date,
        sample_hour
),

final_data_with_category as (
    select
        station_metric_agg.*,

        -- Hourly Category (Based on peak/off-peak periods)
        case
            -- AM Peak
            when
                to_time(station_metric_agg.sample_hour) between to_time('06:00:00') and to_time(
                    '09:59:59'
                )
                then 'am_peak'

            -- Day Off-Peak
            when
                to_time(station_metric_agg.sample_hour) between to_time('10:00:00') and to_time(
                    '14:59:59'
                )
                then 'day_off_peak'

            -- PM Peak
            when
                to_time(station_metric_agg.sample_hour) between to_time('15:00:00') and to_time(
                    '18:59:59'
                )
                then 'pm_peak'

            -- Night Off-Peak (default)
            else 'night_off_peak'
        end as hourly_category,

        -- Weekday/Weekend Category
        case
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) in (1, 2, 3, 4, 5) then 'Weekday'
            else 'Weekend'
        end as weekday_status,

        -- Day of the Week Name
        case
            when station_metric_agg.sample_date is null then 'No Date'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 0 then 'Sunday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 1 then 'Monday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 2 then 'Tuesday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 3 then 'Wednesday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 4 then 'Thursday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 5 then 'Friday'
            when dayofweek(station_metric_agg.sample_date) = 6 then 'Saturday'
            else 'Unknown' -- This should not normally happen
        end as weekday
    from station_metric_agg
)

select
    *,
    extract(hour from sample_hour) as hour_day
from final_data_with_category

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.name: performance__agg_yearly_city
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.description:
yearly aggregation of volume,speed, and occupancy along with delays and lost productivity by
across city. This metrics will measure the yearly performance
by city. This can be used to understand the SHS performance
in perspective of spatial variation.

CITY.name: CITY
CITY.description: The city number where the VDS is located, if available.

YEARLY_SPEED_AVG.name: YEARLY_SPEED_AVG
YEARLY_SPEED_AVG.description: flow weighted speed over the sample year.

YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM.name: YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM
YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM.description: The sum of the flow values over the sample period across city.

YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG.name: YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG
YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG.description: The average of the occupancy values over the sample period across city.

YEARLY_VMT.name: YEARLY_VMT
YEARLY_VMT.description:
The sum of the miles of freeway driven by each vehicle in a given year and
a given city.

YEARLY_VHT.name: YEARLY_VHT
YEARLY_VHT.description:
Vehicle Hours Travelled (VHT) is calculated in a given a year and a
given city. VHT is the amount of time spent by all of the
vehicles on the freeway in a given year.

YEARLY_TTI.name: YEARLY_TTI
YEARLY_TTI.description:
The Travel Time Index (TTI) is the ratio of the average travel time for all users
across a region to the free-flow travel time across city.
The free-flow travel time is taken to be the time to traverse the link when traveling at 60MPH.
For loop-based performance measures, the TTI is simply the free-flow speed divided by the
performance measure Q.

YEARLY_Q_VALUE.name: YEARLY_Q_VALUE
YEARLY_Q_VALUE.description:
Q is the sum of the VMT in a spatial and temporal region divided by the sum of
the VHT in the same region in a given year across city.
For a single location its interpretation is the average speed.

DELAY.name: DELAY
DELAY.description:
yearly delay for different threshold of congestion speed such as 35, 40, 45, 50, 55 and 60 mph
across city.
The Delay performance metric is the amount of additional time spent by the
vehicles on a section of road due to congestion.

LOST_PRODUCTIVITY.name: LOST_PRODUCTIVITY
LOST_PRODUCTIVITY.description:
This is the monthly lost productivity that is simply sum up all days
lost productivity in a given month of the year timeperiod across city.
The lost Productivity perfromance metric is
the number of lane-mile-month on the freeway lost due to operating under congested
conditions instead of under free-flow conditions.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.raw_code:
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

-- read the volume, occupancy and speed daily level data
with station_daily_data as (
    select
        *,
        year(sample_date) as sample_year
    from {{ ref('int_performance__station_metrics_agg_daily') }}
),

-- now aggregate daily volume, occupancy and speed to weekly
spatial_metrics as (
    select
        city,
        sample_year,
        sum(daily_volume) as yearly_volume_sum,
        avg(daily_occupancy) as yearly_occupancy_avg,
        sum(daily_volume * daily_speed) / nullifzero(sum(daily_volume)) as yearly_speed_avg,
        sum(daily_vmt) as yearly_vmt,
        sum(daily_vht) as yearly_vht,
        yearly_vmt / nullifzero(yearly_vht) as yearly_q_value,
        -- travel time
        60 / nullifzero(yearly_q_value) as yearly_tti,
        {% for value in var("V_t") %}
            sum(delay_{{ value }}_mph)
                as delay_{{ value }}_mph
            {% if not loop.last %}
                ,
            {% endif %}

        {% endfor %},
        {% for value in var("V_t") %}
            sum(lost_productivity_{{ value }}_mph)
                as lost_productivity_{{ value }}_mph
            {% if not loop.last %}
                ,
            {% endif %}

        {% endfor %}
    from station_daily_data
    group by
        city, sample_year
)

select * from spatial_metrics

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.raw_code:
with daily_bottleneck_delay as (
    select
        station_id,
        sample_date,
        time_shift,
        cast(district as int) as district,
        station_type,
        freeway,
        direction,
        absolute_postmile,
        daily_time_shift_duration,
        daily_time_shift_bottleneck_extent,
        daily_time_shift_spatial_delay_35_mph,
        daily_time_shift_spatial_delay_40_mph,
        daily_time_shift_spatial_delay_45_mph,
        daily_time_shift_spatial_delay_50_mph,
        daily_time_shift_spatial_delay_55_mph,
        daily_time_shift_spatial_delay_60_mph,
        county
    from {{ ref('int_performance__bottleneck_delay_metrics_agg_daily') }}
),

bottleneck_delay_with_county as (
    {{ get_county_name('daily_bottleneck_delay') }}
),

geo as (
    select
        station_id,
        latitude,
        longitude,
        concat(longitude, ',', latitude) as location
    from {{ ref('geo__current_stations') }}
),

bottleneck_delay_county_geo as (
    select
        bottleneck_delay_with_county.*,
        geo.latitude,
        geo.longitude,
        geo.location
    from
        bottleneck_delay_with_county
    inner join
        geo
        on bottleneck_delay_with_county.station_id = geo.station_id
)

select * from bottleneck_delay_county_geo

YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM.name: YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM

YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG.name: YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG

YEARLY_VMT.name: YEARLY_VMT

a given caltrans district.

YEARLY_VHT.name: YEARLY_VHT

YEARLY_TTI.name: YEARLY_TTI
YEARLY_TTI.description:

YEARLY_Q_VALUE.name: YEARLY_Q_VALUE

Q is the sum of the VMT in a spatial and temporal region divided by the sum of
the VHT in the same region in a given year across caltrans district.
For a single location its interpretation is the average speed.

DELAY.name: DELAY
DELAY.description:
yearly delay for different threshold of congestion speed such as 35, 40, 45, 50, 55 and 60 mph
across caltrans district.
The Delay performance metric is the amount of additional time spent by the
vehicles on a section of road due to congestion.

LOST_PRODUCTIVITY.name: LOST_PRODUCTIVITY
LOST_PRODUCTIVITY.description:
This is the monthly lost productivity that is simply sum up all days
lost productivity in a given month of the year timeperiod across caltrans district.
The lost Productivity perfromance metric is
the number of lane-mile-month on the freeway lost due to operating under congested
conditions instead of under free-flow conditions.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.raw_code:
{{ config(materialized='table') }}

-- read the volume, occupancy and speed daily level data
with station_daily_data as (
    select
        *,
        year(sample_date) as sample_year
    from {{ ref('int_performance__station_metrics_agg_daily') }}
),

-- now aggregate daily volume, occupancy and speed to weekly
spatial_metrics as (
    select
        district,
        sample_year,
        sum(daily_volume) as yearly_volume_sum,
        avg(daily_occupancy) as yearly_occupancy_avg,
        sum(daily_volume * daily_speed) / nullifzero(sum(daily_volume)) as yearly_speed_avg,
        sum(daily_vmt) as yearly_vmt,
        sum(daily_vht) as yearly_vht,
        yearly_vmt / nullifzero(yearly_vht) as yearly_q_value,
        -- travel time
        60 / nullifzero(yearly_q_value) as yearly_tti,
        {% for value in var("V_t") %}
            sum(delay_{{ value }}_mph)
                as delay_{{ value }}_mph
            {% if not loop.last %}
                ,
            {% endif %}

        {% endfor %},
        {% for value in var("V_t") %}
            sum(lost_productivity_{{ value }}_mph)
                as lost_productivity_{{ value }}_mph
            {% if not loop.last %}
                ,
            {% endif %}

        {% endfor %}
    from station_daily_data
    group by
        district, sample_year
)

select * from spatial_metrics

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.name: performance__agg_yearly_freeway_type_direction
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.perform.description:
yearly aggregation of volume,speed, and occupancy along with delays and lost productivity by
across freeway, type and direction. This metrics will measure the yearly performance
of the state highway system. This can be used to understand the SHS performance
in perspective of spatial variation.

STATION_TYPE.name: STATION_TYPE
STATION_TYPE.description: Two character string identify the VDS type.

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION
DIRECTION.description: A string indicating the freeway direction of a specific VDS. Directions are N, E, S or W.

FREEWAY.name: FREEWAY
FREEWAY.description: The freeway where the VDS is located.

YEARLY_SPEED_AVG.name: YEARLY_SPEED_AVG
YEARLY_SPEED_AVG.description: flow weighted speed over the sample year.

YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM.name: YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM
YEARLY_VOLUME_SUM.description: The sum of the flow values over the sample period across freeway, direction and type.

YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG.name: YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG
YEARLY_OCCUPANCY_AVG.description: The average of the occupancy values over the sample period across freeway, direction and type.

YEARLY_VMT.name: YEARLY_VMT
YEARLY_VMT.description:
The sum of the miles of freeway driven by each vehicle in a given year and
a given freeway, direction and type.

YEARLY_VHT.name: YEARLY_VHT
YEARLY_VHT.description:
Vehicle Hours Travelled (VHT) is calculated in a given a year and a
given freeway, direction and type. VHT is the amount of time spent by all of the
vehicles on the freeway in a given year.

YEARLY_TTI.name: YEARLY_TTI
YEARLY_TTI.description:
The Travel Time Index (TTI) is the ratio of the average travel time for all users
across a region to the free-flow travel time across freeway, direction
and type. The free-flow travel time is taken to be the time to traverse the link when traveling at 60MPH.
For loop-based performance measures, the TTI is simply the free-flow speed divided by the
performance measure Q.

YEARLY_Q_VALUE.name: YEARLY_Q_VALUE
YEARLY_Q_VALUE.description:
Q is the sum of the VMT in a spatial and temporal region divided by the sum of
the VHT in the same region in a given year across freeway, direction
and type. For a single location its interpretation is the average speed.

DELAY.name: DELAY
DELAY.description:
yearly delay for different threshold of congestion speed such as 35, 40, 45, 50, 55 and 60 mph
across freeway, direction and type.
The Delay performance metric is the amount of additional time spent by the
vehicles on a section of road due to congestion.

LOST_PRODUCTIVITY.name: LOST_PRODUCTIVITY
LOST_PRODUCTIVITY.description:
This is the monthly lost productivity that is simply sum up all days
lost productivity in a given month of the year timeperiod across caltrans district.
The lost Productivity perfromance metric is
the number of lane-mile-month on the freeway lost due to operating under congested
conditions instead of under free-flow conditions.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo__cu.name: geo__current_detectors
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo__cu.compiled_code:
with detector_config as (
    select * from ANALYTICS_PRD.vds.int_vds__detector_config
),

current_detectors as (
    select
        * exclude (_valid_from, _valid_to),
        st_makepoint(longitude, latitude) as geometry
    from detector_config
    where _valid_to is null
),

current_detectorsc as (
    
    with county as (
        select
            county_id,
            lower(county_name) as county
        from ANALYTICS_PRD.clearinghouse.counties
    ),
    station_with_county as (
        select
            current_detectors.* exclude (county),
            c.county
        from current_detectors
        inner join county as c
        on current_detectors.county = c.county_id
    )

    select * from station_with_county

)

select * from current_detectorsc

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.diagnos.name: diagnostics__detector_daily_detail
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.diagnos.description: This model contains detector status data aggregated to the detector level.

station_id.name: station_id
station_id.description: The unique ID of the station.

status.name: status
status.description:
The detector health value based on a series of validations made from
sample counts

sample_date.name: sample_date
sample_date.description: The date associated with raw data samples being counted.

district.name: district
district.description: The Caltrans district for the station.

county.name: county
county.description: The county FIPS code in which the station installed.

city.name: city
city.description: The city FIPS code in which the station is installed.

freeway.name: freeway
freeway.description: The freeway on which the station is installed.

direction.name: direction
direction.description: The direction of travel for the freeway on which the station is installed.

physical_lanes.name: physical_lanes
physical_lanes.description: The number of lanes in the station

state_postmile.name: state_postmile
state_postmile.description: The State postmile for the station.

absolute_postmile.name: absolute_postmile
absolute_postmile.description: The absolute postmile for the station.

detector_id.name: detector_id
detector_id.description: The unique ID of the detector.

sample_ct.name: sample_ct
sample_ct.description:
Counts the number of raw data samples where a lane's volume (flow) and occupancy
values contain any non-null value

zero_vol_ct.name: zero_vol_ct
zero_vol_ct.description:
Counts the number of raw data samples where a lane's volume (flow) value
equals 0

zero_occ_ct.name: zero_occ_ct
zero_occ_ct.description:
Counts the number of raw data samples where a lane's occupancy
value equals 0

zero_vol_pos_occ_ct.name: zero_vol_pos_occ_ct
zero_vol_pos_occ_ct.description:
Counts the number of raw data samples where a lane's volume (flow)
value equals 0 but occupancy is greater than 0

zero_occ_pos_vol_ct.name: zero_occ_pos_vol_ct
zero_occ_pos_vol_ct.description:
Counts the number of raw data samples where a lane's occupancy
value equals 0 but volume (flow) is greater than 0

high_volume_ct.name: high_volume_ct
high_volume_ct.description:
Counts the number of volume (flow) values in a lane that exceeded the high value threshold
detector diagnostic parameter for a station.

high_occupancy_ct.name: high_occupancy_ct
high_occupancy_ct.description:
Counts the number of occupancy values in a lane that exceeded the high value threshold
detector diagnostic parameter for a station.

active_date.name: active_date
active_date.description:
Date a station is considered active based on the meta data configuration file from district.
This value determines what calculations or diagnostics are associated with a station and it's
assocaited detectors on any given date while it is active.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.diagnos.compiled_code:
with

detector_status as (
    select * from ANALYTICS_PRD.diagnostics.int_diagnostics__detector_status
    where sample_date is not null and lane is not null
),

detector_statusc as (
    
    with county as (
        select
            county_id,
            lower(county_name) as county
        from ANALYTICS_PRD.clearinghouse.counties
    ),
    station_with_county as (
        select
            detector_status.* exclude (county),
            c.county
        from detector_status
        inner join county as c
        on detector_status.county = c.county_id
    )

    select * from station_with_county

)

select * from detector_statusc

sample_date.description: The date associated with raw data samples being counted.

absolute_postmile.name: absolute_postmile
absolute_postmile.description: The absolute postmile for the station.

good_detector_count.description: The number of good detectors per day and station.

bad_detector_count.name: bad_detector_count
bad_detector_count.description: The number of bad detectors per day and station.

average_sample_count.name: average_sample_count
average_sample_count.description:
The average number of samples from all detectors reported from.
per station and day

down_or_no_data_count.name: down_or_no_data_count
down_or_no_data_count.description: The number of down or no data bad detectors per day and station.

insufficient_data_count.name: insufficient_data_count
insufficient_data_count.description: The number of bad detectors per day and station that have insufficient data.

card_off_count.name: card_off_count
card_off_count.description: The number of bad detectors per day and station that card was off.

high_val_count.name: high_val_count
high_val_count.description: The number of bad detectors per day and station that had extremely high value.

intermittent_count.description: The number of bad detectors per day and station that had intermittent detector issues.

constant_count.name: constant_count
constant_count.description: The number of bad detectors per day and station that had constant occupancy issues.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.diagnos.raw_code:
{{ config(
    materialized="table",
    unload_partitioning="('year=' || to_varchar(date_part(year, sample_date)) || '/month=' || to_varchar(date_part(month, sample_date)))",
) }}

with

detector_status as (
    select * from {{ ref("int_diagnostics__detector_status") }}
),

detector_status_with_count as (
    select
        district,
        station_id,
        lane,
        station_type,
        sample_date,
        sample_ct,
        count_if(status = 'Good') as good_detector,
        count_if(status != 'Good') as bad_detector,
        count_if(status = 'Down/No Data') as down_or_no_data,
        count_if(status = 'Insufficient Data') as insufficient_data,
        count_if(status = 'Card Off') as card_off,
        count_if(status = 'High Val') as high_val,
        count_if(status = 'Intermittent') as intermittent,
        count_if(status = 'Constant') as constant
    from detector_status
    group by district, station_id, lane, station_type, sample_date, sample_ct
),

detector_status_by_station as (
    select
        district,
        station_id,
        station_type,
        sample_date,
        count(*) as detector_count,
        round(avg(sample_ct)) as average_sample_count,
        sum(good_detector) as good_detector_count,
        sum(bad_detector) as bad_detector_count,
        sum(down_or_no_data) as down_or_no_data_count,
        sum(insufficient_data) as insufficient_data_count,
        sum(card_off) as card_off_count,
        sum(high_val) as high_val_count,
        sum(intermittent) as intermittent_count,
        sum(constant) as constant_count
    from detector_status_with_count
    group by district, station_id, station_type, sample_date
),

dmeta as (
    select * from {{ ref('int_vds__station_config') }}
),

detector_status_by_station_with_metadata as (
    select
        dsbs.*,
        dmeta.state_postmile,
        dmeta.absolute_postmile,
        dmeta.latitude,
        dmeta.longitude,
        dmeta.physical_lanes,
        dmeta.county,
        dmeta.city,
        dmeta.freeway,
        dmeta.direction,
        dmeta.length
    from detector_status_by_station as dsbs
    inner join dmeta
        on
            dsbs.station_id = dmeta.station_id
            and dsbs.sample_date >= dmeta._valid_from
            and (
                dsbs.sample_date < dmeta._valid_to
                or dmeta._valid_to is null
            )
    where dsbs.sample_date is not null
),

detector_status_by_station_with_metadatac as (
    {{ get_county_name('detector_status_by_station_with_metadata') }}
)

select * from detector_status_by_station_with_metadatac

monthly_good_detector_count.name: monthly_good_detector_count
monthly_good_detector_count.description: The number of good detectors per month and station.

monthly_bad_detector_count.name: monthly_bad_detector_count
monthly_bad_detector_count.description: The number of bad detectors per month and station.

down_or_no_data_count.name: down_or_no_data_count
down_or_no_data_count.description: The number of down or no data bad detectors per month and station.

insufficient_data_count.name: insufficient_data_count
insufficient_data_count.description: The number of bad detectors per month and station that have insufficient data.

card_off_count.name: card_off_count
card_off_count.description: The number of bad detectors per month and station that card was off.

high_val_count.name: high_val_count
high_val_count.description: The number of bad detectors per month and station that had extremely high value.

intermittent_count.name: intermittent_count
intermittent_count.description: The number of bad detectors per month and station that had intermittent detector issues.

constant_count.name: constant_count
constant_count.description: The number of bad detectors per month and station that had constant occupancy issues.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.diagnos.raw_code:
{{ config(
    materialized="table",
    unload_partitioning="('year=' || to_varchar(date_part(year, sample_month)) || '/month=' || to_varchar(date_part(month, sample_month)))",
) }}

with detector_daily_status as (
    select
        *,
        DATE_TRUNC(month, sample_date) as sample_month,
        ROW_NUMBER() over (partition by sample_month, station_id order by sample_date desc) as rn
    from {{ ref('diagnostics__detector_daily_by_station') }}
),

detector_monthly_status_by_station as (
    select
        sample_month,
        station_id,
        county,
        MAX(case when rn = 1 then district end) as district,
        MAX(case when rn = 1 then state_postmile end) as state_postmile,
        MAX(case when rn = 1 then absolute_postmile end) as absolute_postmile,
        MAX(case when rn = 1 then latitude end) as latitude,
        MAX(case when rn = 1 then longitude end) as longitude,
        MAX(case when rn = 1 then station_type end) as station_type,
        MAX(case when rn = 1 then city end) as city,
        MAX(case when rn = 1 then freeway end) as freeway,
        MAX(case when rn = 1 then direction end) as direction,
        SUM(detector_count) as monthly_detector_count,
        SUM(good_detector_count) as monthly_good_detector_count,
        SUM(bad_detector_count) as monthly_bad_detector_count,
        SUM(down_or_no_data_count) as down_or_no_data_count,
        SUM(insufficient_data_count) as insufficient_data_count,
        SUM(card_off_count) as card_off_count,
        SUM(high_val_count) as high_val_count,
        SUM(intermittent_count) as intermittent_count,
        SUM(constant_count) as constant_count
    from
        detector_daily_status
    group by
        station_id,
        sample_month,
        county
)

select * from detector_monthly_status_by_station

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_cle.name: stg_clearinghouse__station_raw
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_cle.description:
Vehicle detector station 30-second raw data from the
[PeMS Clearinghouse feeds](https://pems.dot.ca.gov/feeds/clhouse/).

ID.name: ID
ID.description: Unique VDS (station identifier) value. Use this value to cross-reference with Metadata files.

SAMPLE_DATE.name: SAMPLE_DATE
SAMPLE_DATE.description: 30 second raw data sample time as reported by the field element as YYYY/MM/DD.

SAMPLE_TIMESTAMP.name: SAMPLE_TIMESTAMP
SAMPLE_TIMESTAMP.description: 30 second raw data sample time as reported by the field element as YYYY/MM/DD HH24:MI:SS.

LANE.name: LANE
LANE.description: The lane number for the detector, starting from 1.

VOLUME.name: VOLUME
VOLUME.description:
Number of vehicle that passed over the detector during the sample period for the lane.
Units are Vehicles/sample period.

OCCUPANCY.name: OCCUPANCY
OCCUPANCY.description: Occupancy of the lane during the sample period expressed as a decimal number between 0 and 1.

SPEED.name: SPEED
SPEED.description:
Speed in the lane as measured by the detector in miles/hour.
Empty if the detector does not report speed.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_cle.name: stg_clearinghouse__station_status
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_cle.description:
TMDD Status data from PeMS Clearinghouse. This seems to be the main source of detector
ID to station ID mapping.

FILENAME.name: FILENAME
FILENAME.description: The name of the file in the clearinghouse where the metdata was obtained from.

META_DATE.name: META_DATE
META_DATE.description: The date of the XML metadata file

STATION_ID.name: STATION_ID
STATION_ID.description: The station ID

DETECTOR_ID.name: DETECTOR_ID
DETECTOR_ID.description:
The detector ID. In general, there can be several detectors for a single
station, corresponding to different lanes of traffic.

DETECTOR_STATUS.name: DETECTOR_STATUS
DETECTOR_STATUS.description:
Detector status as reported by the XML file. Probably not useful when determining
*actual* status of the detector.

LAST_UPDATE_TIME.name: LAST_UPDATE_TIME
LAST_UPDATE_TIME.description: The last updated time. This seems to be empty for the whole dataset.

LANE_NUMBER.name: LANE_NUMBER
LANE_NUMBER.description:
The lane number for the detector. This seems to be reported as
1, 10, 100, 1000, 10000, etc for each successive lane, rather than counting up
from one.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_cle.raw_code:
{{ config(materialized="table") }}
{% set
  date_re='clhouse/status/d\\\\d{2}/\\\\d{4}/\\\\d{2}/d(\\\\d{2})_tmdd_meta_(\\\\d{4})_(\\\\d{2})_(\\\\d{2}).xml'
%}
/*
Helpful article for flattening XML:
https://community.snowflake.com/s/article/HOW-TO-QUERY-NESTED-XML-DATA-IN-SNOWFLAKE
*/
SELECT
    STATUS.FILENAME,
    DATE_FROM_PARTS(
        REGEXP_SUBSTR(STATUS.FILENAME, '{{ date_re }}', 1, 1, '', 2)::INT,
        REGEXP_SUBSTR(STATUS.FILENAME, '{{ date_re }}', 1, 1, '', 3)::INT,
        REGEXP_SUBSTR(STATUS.FILENAME, '{{ date_re }}', 1, 1, '', 4)::INT
    ) AS META_DATE,
    REGEXP_SUBSTR(STATUS.FILENAME, '{{ date_re }}', 1, 1, '', 1)::INT AS DISTRICT,
    XMLGET(STATUS.CONTENT, 'station-id'):"$"::VARCHAR AS STATION_ID,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'), 'detector-id'):"$"::VARCHAR AS DETECTOR_ID,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'), 'detector-name'):"$"::VARCHAR AS DETECTOR_NAME,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'), 'detector-status'):"$"::VARCHAR AS DETECTOR_STATUS,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'), 'tmdd:last-update-time'):"$"::VARCHAR AS LAST_UPDATE_TIME,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(LANE.VALUE, 'detection-lane-item'), 'lane-number'):"$"::INT AS LANE_NUMBER
FROM
    {{ source("clearinghouse", "station_status") }} AS STATUS,
    /*
    It's not 100% clear that these flattening operations are necessary. The structure of
    the XML documents suggest that there can be multiple detectors in a 'detector-list',
    and multiple lanes in a 'detection-lane'. However, there seem to be few (if any) instances
    of entries where that is the case. So these flattenings are defensive.
    */
    LATERAL FLATTEN(STATUS.CONTENT:"$") AS DETECTOR,
    LATERAL FLATTEN(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'):"$") AS LANE
WHERE
    GET(DETECTOR.VALUE, '@') = 'detector-list'
    AND GET(LANE.VALUE, '@') = 'detection-lane'
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_cle.compiled_code:
/*
Helpful article for flattening XML:
https://community.snowflake.com/s/article/HOW-TO-QUERY-NESTED-XML-DATA-IN-SNOWFLAKE
*/
SELECT
    STATUS.FILENAME,
    DATE_FROM_PARTS(
        REGEXP_SUBSTR(STATUS.FILENAME, 'clhouse/status/d\\d{2}/\\d{4}/\\d{2}/d(\\d{2})_tmdd_meta_(\\d{4})_(\\d{2})_(\\d{2}).xml', 1, 1, '', 2)::INT,
        REGEXP_SUBSTR(STATUS.FILENAME, 'clhouse/status/d\\d{2}/\\d{4}/\\d{2}/d(\\d{2})_tmdd_meta_(\\d{4})_(\\d{2})_(\\d{2}).xml', 1, 1, '', 3)::INT,
        REGEXP_SUBSTR(STATUS.FILENAME, 'clhouse/status/d\\d{2}/\\d{4}/\\d{2}/d(\\d{2})_tmdd_meta_(\\d{4})_(\\d{2})_(\\d{2}).xml', 1, 1, '', 4)::INT
    ) AS META_DATE,
    REGEXP_SUBSTR(STATUS.FILENAME, 'clhouse/status/d\\d{2}/\\d{4}/\\d{2}/d(\\d{2})_tmdd_meta_(\\d{4})_(\\d{2})_(\\d{2}).xml', 1, 1, '', 1)::INT AS DISTRICT,
    XMLGET(STATUS.CONTENT, 'station-id'):"$"::VARCHAR AS STATION_ID,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'), 'detector-id'):"$"::VARCHAR AS DETECTOR_ID,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'), 'detector-name'):"$"::VARCHAR AS DETECTOR_NAME,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'), 'detector-status'):"$"::VARCHAR AS DETECTOR_STATUS,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'), 'tmdd:last-update-time'):"$"::VARCHAR AS LAST_UPDATE_TIME,
    XMLGET(XMLGET(LANE.VALUE, 'detection-lane-item'), 'lane-number'):"$"::INT AS LANE_NUMBER
FROM
    RAW_PRD.clearinghouse.station_status AS STATUS,
    /*
    It's not 100% clear that these flattening operations are necessary. The structure of
    the XML documents suggest that there can be multiple detectors in a 'detector-list',
    and multiple lanes in a 'detection-lane'. However, there seem to be few (if any) instances
    of entries where that is the case. So these flattenings are defensive.
    */
    LATERAL FLATTEN(STATUS.CONTENT:"$") AS DETECTOR,
    LATERAL FLATTEN(XMLGET(DETECTOR.VALUE, 'detector'):"$") AS LANE
WHERE
    GET(DETECTOR.VALUE, '@') = 'detector-list'
    AND GET(LANE.VALUE, '@') = 'detection-lane'

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_cle.name: stg_clearinghouse__station_meta
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_cle.description: Vehicle detector station metadata from the [PeMS Clearinghouse feeds](https://pems.dot.ca.gov/feeds/clhouse/).

FILENAME.name: FILENAME
FILENAME.description: The name of the file in the clearinghouse where the metdata was obtained from.

ID.name: ID
ID.description:
An integer value that uniquely indenties the Station Metadata.
Use this value to 'join' other clearinghouse files that contain Station Metadata

ABSOLUTE_POSTMILE.name: ABSOLUTE_POSTMILE
ABSOLUTE_POSTMILE.description: Absolute postmile value along a partilcuar state route where the VDS is located

CITY.name: CITY
CITY.description: The city number where the VDS is located, if available

COUNTY.name: COUNTY
COUNTY.description: The unique number that identifies the county that contains a specific VDS within PeMS.

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION
DIRECTION.description: A string indicating the freeway direction of a specific VDS. Directions are N, E, S or W.

DISTRICT.name: DISTRICT
DISTRICT.description: The district in which the VDS is located. Values are 1-12

FREEWAY.name: FREEWAY
FREEWAY.description: The freeway where the VDS is located

LANES.name: LANES
LANES.description: Total number of lanes for a specific VDS

LENGTH.name: LENGTH
LENGTH.description: The length associated with a VDS

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.description: A descriptive name assocaited with a VDS

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.description: Two character string identify the VDS type

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_cle.raw_code:

    ID,

    DIR AS DIRECTION,
    DISTRICT,

    NAME

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_geo.name: stg_geo_reference__county_boundaries
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_geo.description:
This polygon feature class represents the California county boundaries. Based on TIGER/Line 2010 county
files, the Caltrans districts and California county name abbreviation were added to this layer for
Caltrans internal GIS application. [Data
source](https://gisdata-caltrans.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/111030d0d67e49d789080c47d9e4e618_0/about)

GEO_ID.name: GEO_ID
GEO_ID.description: 2020 Census state and county FIPS codes concatenated.

LATITUDE.name: LATITUDE
LATITUDE.description: The latitude of the county boundary.

LONGITUDE.name: LONGITUDE
LONGITUDE.description: The longitude of the county boundary.

DISTRICT.name: DISTRICT
DISTRICT.description: The district in which the county boundary is located. Values are 1-12.

AREA.name: AREA
AREA.description: The area of the county boundary.

LENGTH.name: LENGTH
LENGTH.description: The length of the county boundary.

GEOMETRY.name: GEOMETRY
GEOMETRY.description: The geometry of the county boundary.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_geo.name: stg_geo_reference__shn_lines
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_geo.description:
StateHighways are state highways based upon the Caltrans Linear Referencing System. Each record in this
layer represents a highway segment where the county, route, postmile prefix, and postmile suffix are the
same. Each segment has a begin and end postmile. This geometry is based upon the extraction from TSN on
10 October 2022.

Each segment is coded with the district, county, route, postmile prefix (if any), begin postmile, end
postmile, and postmile suffix (if any). One additional field - AlignCode - should be understood by the
user for effective use of this data set (refer to the Entity and Attribute Information section of this
metadata).

AlignCode indicates if the state highway segment is on a Right alignment, Left alignment, Right side of
an independent alignment or Left side of an independent alignment. Where TSN (Transportation System
Network - the source database) considers most state highways to have just a centerline, the underlying
LRS (Linear Referencing System) linework that these segments are based upon has a line for each direction.

On undivided highways the right and left lines are identical. On divided highways the right and left are
separated, but TSN treats the segment as a single centerline. In the last case where highways are not only
divided but also have different lengths (where the right and left carriageway diverge around physical
obstacles or are separated onto different one-way streets) the underlying linework has dual carriageways,
and TSN treats the segment as an "independent alignment".  In some cases (especially for small-scale
mapping), it may be appropriate to display linework for just one alignment (e.g. Right alignment), and only
for the other side where TSN indicates independent right and left alignments. In other cases (e.g.
large-scale mapping), it may be appropriate to display postmiles for both alignments, regardless of how TSN
treats the segment.

The bOdometer and eOdometer fields represent the actual distance in miles from the start of the highway to
the begin and end of each highway section.  This is in contrast to the begin and end postmile values, which
no longer represent these values as each highway is realigned (and made longer or shorter) over time. [Data
source](https://gisdata-caltrans.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/77f2d7ba94e040a78bfbe36feb6279da_0/about)

COUNTY.name: COUNTY
COUNTY.description: Three chracter county code.

DISTRICT.name: DISTRICT
DISTRICT.description: The district in which the highway is located. Values are 1-12.

B_ODOMETER.name: B_ODOMETER
B_ODOMETER.description:
Represents the distance in miles from the start of the highway to
the beginning and end of each highway section.

E_ODOMETER.name: E_ODOMETER
E_ODOMETER.description:
Represents the distance in miles from the start of the highway to
the beginning and end of each highway section.

ROUTE_TYPE.name: ROUTE_TYPE
ROUTE_TYPE.description: The type of route of the highway. Values are State, US, and Interstate.

DIRECTION.name: DIRECTION
DIRECTION.description:
The direction of the highway.
Values are NB, SB, EB, and WB (north-, south-, east-, and west-bound).

LENGTH.name: LENGTH
LENGTH.description: The length of the state highway.

GEOMETRY.name: GEOMETRY
GEOMETRY.description: The geometry of the state highway.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_geo.raw_code:
select

    /* RteSuffix was dropped because less than 1% of it
    was filled and as such it didn't seem very useful.
    Route is very similar to RouteS and was therefore dropped
    as well. */

    "RouteS" as ROUTE_WITH_SUFFIX,
    "PMRouteID" as PM_ROUTE_ID,
    "County" as COUNTY,
    "District" as DISTRICT,
    "PMPrefix" as PM_PREFIX,
    "bPM" as BPM,
    "ePM" as EPM,
    "PMSuffix" as PM_SUFFIX,
    "bPMc" as BPMC,
    "ePMc" as EPMC,
    "bOdometer" as B_ODOMETER,
    "eOdometer" as E_ODOMETER,
    "AlignCode" as ALIGN_CODE,
    "RouteType" as ROUTE_TYPE,
    "Direction" as DIRECTION,
    "Shape__Length" as LENGTH,
    "geometry" as GEOMETRY

from {{ source('geo_reference', 'shn_lines') }}

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_geo.name: stg_geo_reference__districts
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_geo.description:
The District feature class is a polygon coverage representing the California Department of Transportation
(Caltrans) district boundaries. There are 12 Caltrans Districts in California. Caltrans also has Regions
as higher level management areas. The names of Caltrans Regions are included in the layer attribute table.

The District boundary definitions are primarily based on the California county boundaries. The Board of
Equalization (BOE) County and City Boundary web service is the authoritative source of County boundaries
and this was used to digitize the district boundaries based on the data from March 2023. This data is
solely for informational purposes. District 9 has been operating independent of the Central Region since
November 1, 2015. Kern County remains the only split county in the state, between Districts 6 and 9
respectively. The BOE data was also used to create the district boundary layers along the coastline.
[Data source](https://gisdata-caltrans.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/0144574f750f4ccc88749004aca6eb0c_0/about)

DISTRICT.name: DISTRICT
DISTRICT.description: The territorial division (as for administrative or electoral purposes). Values are 1-12.

REGION.name: REGION
REGION.description: The administrative area of the district.

model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_geo.raw_code:
select

    DISTRICT,
    "Region" as REGION,
    "Shape__Area" as AREA,
    "Shape__Length" as LENGTH,
    "geometry" as GEOMETRY

from {{ source('geo_reference', 'districts') }}
model.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.stg_geo.compiled_code:
select

    DISTRICT,
    "Region" as REGION,
    "Shape__Area" as AREA,
    "Shape__Length" as LENGTH,
    "geometry" as GEOMETRY

from RAW_PRD.geo_reference.districts

seed.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.detector.description:
Data comes from the district metadata file.
24 static records on detector and station type combinations and their descriptions.
Reference: PeMSSampleReports.docx

DETECTOR_TYPE.name: DETECTOR_TYPE
DETECTOR_TYPE.description: A two character code indicating the type of detector.

STATION_TYPE.name: STATION_TYPE
STATION_TYPE.description: A two character code indicating the type of station.

DESCRIPTION.name: DESCRIPTION
DESCRIPTION.description: The full name of the detector and station codes.

seed.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.states.name: states
seed.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.states.description:
The states seed file contains the FIPS code and other associated location information
about the state of California used in PeMS

STATE_ID.name: STATE_ID
STATE_ID.description: The California FIPS code which is 6

STATE_LATITUDE.name: STATE_LATITUDE
STATE_LATITUDE.description: The latitude of California used in PeMS  (Standard projection of NAD 83)

STATE_LONGITUDE.name: STATE_LONGITUDE
STATE_LONGITUDE.description: The longitude of California used in PeMS (Standard projection of NAD 83)

seed.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.district.name: districts
seed.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.district.description:
The districts seed file contains the 12 Caltrans Districts in California. The
District boundary definitions are primarily based on the California county
boundaries.

DISTRICT_ID.name: DISTRICT_ID
DISTRICT_ID.description: The Caltrans district code associated with a region. Values are 1 - 12.

DISTRICT_NAME.name: DISTRICT_NAME
DISTRICT_NAME.description: The district name associated with the district code

STATE_ID.name: STATE_ID
STATE_ID.description: The California FIPS code which is 6

DISTRICT_LATITUDE.name: DISTRICT_LATITUDE
DISTRICT_LATITUDE.description:
The latitude of a district used in PeMS associated with the approximate
mid point of the district (Standard projection of NAD 83)

DISTRICT_LONGITUDE.name: DISTRICT_LONGITUDE
DISTRICT_LONGITUDE.description:
The longitude of a district used in PeMS associated with the approximate
mid point of the district (Standard projection of NAD 83)

seed.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.counties.name: counties
seed.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.counties.description:
The counties seed file contains the relationships between counties and their associated
Caltrans Districts. The file also contains full county names and abbreviated naming
convention used in Caltrans. Additional information regarding FIPS codes can be found at:
https://www.census.gov/library/reference/code-lists/ansi.html

COUNTY_ID.name: COUNTY_ID
COUNTY_ID.description: FIPS numeric code associated with a county

DISTRICT_ID.name: DISTRICT_ID
DISTRICT_ID.description: The Caltrans district that the county is located in (values are 1-12)

COUNTY_NAME.name: COUNTY_NAME
COUNTY_NAME.description: The full name of the county

NATIVE_ID.name: NATIVE_ID
NATIVE_ID.description: The 2-3 character abbreviation for the County used in Caltrans

seed.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.diagnost.name: diagnostic_threshold_values
seed.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.diagnost.description:
PeMS uses 30-second or 5-minute data to calculate performance measures.
59 static records that contain diagnostic threshold values by diagnostic threshold set and detector type.

Reference:
  - [Detector Diagnostics](https://pems.dot.ca.gov/?dnode=Help&content=help_calc#diag)
  - [PEMS User Guide](https://pems.dot.ca.gov/Papers/PeMS_Intro_User_Guide_v6.pdf)
  - PeMSSampleReports.docx

DT_SET_ID.name: DT_SET_ID
DT_SET_ID.description: Detector Threshold Set assigned to Station.

DT_METHOD.name: DT_METHOD
DT_METHOD.description: Detector deployment location in the field

DT_NAME.name: DT_NAME
DT_NAME.description: Name of the diagnostic test condition associated with the station

DT_VALUE.name: DT_VALUE
DT_VALUE.description: Threshold value associated with the diagnostic test condition

source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.cleari.name: station_meta
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.cleari.source_description: Vehicle Detector Station (VDS) (aka Station) related data obtained from the PeMS clearinghouse
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.cleari.description:
Contains Vehicle Detector Station (VDS) metadata related to a specific VDS
which are also typically referred to as Stations.

ABS_PM.name: ABS_PM
ABS_PM.description: Absolute postmile value along a partilcuar state route where the VDS is located

CITY.name: CITY
CITY.description: The city number where the VDS is located, if available

COUNTY.name: COUNTY
COUNTY.description: The unique number that identifies the county that contains a specific VDS within PeMS.

DIR.name: DIR
DIR.description: A string indicating the freeway direction of a specific VDS. Directions are N, E, S or W.

DISTRICT.name: DISTRICT
DISTRICT.description: The district in which the VDS is located. Values are 1-12

FILENAME.name: FILENAME
FILENAME.description: The name of the file in the clearinghouse where the metdata was obtained from.

FWY.name: FWY
FWY.description: The freeway where the VDS is located

ID.name: ID
ID.description:
An integer value that uniquely indenties the Station Metadata.
Use this value to 'join' other clearinghouse files that contain Station Metadata

LANES.name: LANES
LANES.description: Total number of lanes for a specific VDS

LENGTH.name: LENGTH
LENGTH.description: The length associated with a VDS

NAME.name: NAME
NAME.description: A descriptive name assocaited with a VDS

TYPE.name: TYPE
TYPE.description: Two character string identify the VDS type

source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.cleari.name: station_raw
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.cleari.source_description: Vehicle Detector Station (VDS) (aka Station) related data obtained from the PeMS clearinghouse
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.cleari.description:
Raw detector data as reported every 30 seconds by the district.
Each line contains the sample timestamp and Station ID followed by flow,
occupancy and speed for each lane. Note that occupancy and/or speed may be empty
depending on the measurement capabilities of the detectors.

FILENAME.name: FILENAME
FILENAME.description: The name of the file in the clearinghouse where the data was obtained from.

ID.name: ID
ID.description: Unique VDS (station identifier) value. Use this value to cross-reference with Metadata files.

SAMPLE_DATE.name: SAMPLE_DATE
SAMPLE_DATE.description: 30 second raw data sample time as reported by the field element as YYYY/MM/DD.

SAMPLE_TIMESTAMP.name: SAMPLE_TIMESTAMP
SAMPLE_TIMESTAMP.description: 30 second raw data sample time as reported by the field element as YYYY/MM/DD HH24:MI:SS.

FLOW_1.name: FLOW_1
FLOW_1.description:
Number of vehicle that passed over the detector during the sample period for lane 1.
Units are Vehicles/sample period.

FLOW_2.name: FLOW_2
FLOW_2.description:
Number of vehicle that passed over the detector during the sample period for lane 2.
Units are Vehicles/sample period.

FLOW_3.name: FLOW_3
FLOW_3.description:
Number of vehicle that passed over the detector during the sample period for lane 3.
Units are Vehicles/sample period.

FLOW_4.name: FLOW_4
FLOW_4.description:
Number of vehicle that passed over the detector during the sample period for lane 4.
Units are Vehicles/sample period.

FLOW_5.name: FLOW_5
FLOW_5.description:
Number of vehicle that passed over the detector during the sample period for lane 5.
Units are Vehicles/sample period.

FLOW_6.name: FLOW_6
FLOW_6.description:
Number of vehicle that passed over the detector during the sample period for lane 6.
Units are Vehicles/sample period.

FLOW_7.name: FLOW_7
FLOW_7.description:
Number of vehicle that passed over the detector during the sample period for lane 7.
Units are Vehicles/sample period.

FLOW_8.name: FLOW_8
FLOW_8.description:
Number of vehicle that passed over the detector during the sample period for lane 8.
Units are Vehicles/sample period.

OCCUPANCY_1.name: OCCUPANCY_1
OCCUPANCY_1.description: Occupancy of the lane 1 during the sample period expressed as a decimal number between 0 and 1.

OCCUPANCY_2.name: OCCUPANCY_2
OCCUPANCY_2.description: Occupancy of the lane 2 during the sample period expressed as a decimal number between 0 and 1.

OCCUPANCY_3.name: OCCUPANCY_3
OCCUPANCY_3.description: Occupancy of the lane 3 during the sample period expressed as a decimal number between 0 and 1.

OCCUPANCY_4.name: OCCUPANCY_4
OCCUPANCY_4.description: Occupancy of the lane 4 during the sample period expressed as a decimal number between 0 and 1.

OCCUPANCY_5.name: OCCUPANCY_5
OCCUPANCY_5.description: Occupancy of the lane 5 during the sample period expressed as a decimal number between 0 and 1.

OCCUPANCY_6.name: OCCUPANCY_6
OCCUPANCY_6.description: Occupancy of the lane 6 during the sample period expressed as a decimal number between 0 and 1.

OCCUPANCY_7.name: OCCUPANCY_7
OCCUPANCY_7.description: Occupancy of the lane 7 during the sample period expressed as a decimal number between 0 and 1.

OCCUPANCY_8.name: OCCUPANCY_8
OCCUPANCY_8.description: Occupancy of the lane 8 during the sample period expressed as a decimal number between 0 and 1.

SPEED_1.name: SPEED_1
SPEED_1.description:
Speed in lane 1 as measured by the detector in miles/hour.
Empty if the detector does not report speed.

SPEED_2.name: SPEED_2
SPEED_2.description:
Speed in lane 2 as measured by the detector in miles/hour.
Empty if the detector does not report speed.

SPEED_3.name: SPEED_3
SPEED_3.description:
Speed in lane 3 as measured by the detector in miles/hour.
Empty if the detector does not report speed.

SPEED_4.name: SPEED_4
SPEED_4.description:
Speed in lane 4 as measured by the detector in miles/hour.
Empty if the detector does not report speed.

SPEED_5.name: SPEED_5
SPEED_5.description:
Speed in lane 5 as measured by the detector in miles/hour.
Empty if the detector does not report speed.

SPEED_6.name: SPEED_6
SPEED_6.description:
Speed in lane 6 as measured by the detector in miles/hour.
Empty if the detector does not report speed.

SPEED_7.name: SPEED_7
SPEED_7.description:
Speed in lane 7 as measured by the detector in miles/hour.
Empty if the detector does not report speed.

SPEED_8.name: SPEED_8
SPEED_8.description:
Speed in lane 8 as measured by the detector in miles/hour.
Empty if the detector does not report speed.

source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.cleari.description:
XML Status data from the [PeMS Clearinghouse Feeds](https://pems.dot.ca.gov/feeds/clhouse/).
An example is [here](https://pems.dot.ca.gov/feeds/clhouse/d07/2022/07/meta/d07_tmdd_meta_2022_07_15.xml).

Vehicle Detector Station (VDS) (aka Station) related data obtained via data relay from
db96 within the Caltrans network.
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.db96.v.description:
30-second resolution VDS data from DB96, a legacy Oracle database within Caltrans'
on-premise network.

FILENAME.name: FILENAME
FILENAME.description: The name of the parquet file uploaded to S3 and loaded to Snowflake via Snowpipe.

META.name: META
META.description: Metadata from the data relay scripts.

SAMPLE_DATE.name: SAMPLE_DATE
SAMPLE_DATE.description: The date on which this data was collected (in America/Los_Angeles timezone).

SAMPLE_TIME.name: SAMPLE_TIME
SAMPLE_TIME.description:
The timestamp for the 30-second resolution data. This is a timezone-naive timestamp,
as it is also that in the source system. However, it seems to be in America/Los_Angeles.

VDS_ID.name: VDS_ID
VDS_ID.description: The ID for the VDS station.

Vehicle Detector Station (VDS) (aka Station) related data obtained via data relay from
db96 within the Caltrans network.
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.db96.c.description:
Configuration metadata for VDS controllers. There may be multiple
stations hooked up to a single controller. For example, the same
controller might have a station for the mainline as well as a station
for an onramp.

meta.description: Metadata from the data relay scripts.

controller_id.description: The unique ID of the controller.

district_id.name: district_id

county_id.name: county_id
county_id.description: The county FIPS code in which the controller is installed.

city_id.name: city_id
city_id.description: The city FIPS code in which the controller is installed.

freeway_id.name: freeway_id
freeway_id.description: The freeway on which the controller is installed.

freeway_dir.name: freeway_dir
freeway_dir.description: The direction of travel for the freeway on which the controller is installed.

controller_id.description: The unique ID of the controller.

source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo_re.name: county_boundaries
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo_re.source_description: Geographic tables to be used in creating maps and other GIS-related visualizations.
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo_re.description:
This polygon feature class represents the California county boundaries. Based on TIGER/Line 2010 county
files, the Caltrans districts and California county name abbreviation were added to this layer for
Caltrans internal GIS application. [Data
source](https://gisdata-caltrans.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/111030d0d67e49d789080c47d9e4e618_0/about)

GEOID10.name: GEOID10
GEOID10.description: 2020 Census state and county FIPS codes concatenated.

INTPTLAT10.name: INTPTLAT10
INTPTLAT10.description: The latitude of the county boundary.

INTPTLON10.name: INTPTLON10
INTPTLON10.description: The longitude of the county boundary.

DISTRICT.name: DISTRICT
DISTRICT.description: The district in which the county boundary is located. Values are 1-12.

Shape__Area.name: Shape__Area
Shape__Area.description: The area of the county boundary.

Shape__Length.name: Shape__Length
Shape__Length.description: The length of the county boundary.

geometry.name: geometry
geometry.description: The geometry of the county boundary.

source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo_re.name: districts
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo_re.source_description: Geographic tables to be used in creating maps and other GIS-related visualizations.
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo_re.description:
The District feature class is a polygon coverage representing the California Department of Transportation
(Caltrans) district boundaries. There are 12 Caltrans Districts in California. Caltrans also has Regions
as higher level management areas. The names of Caltrans Regions are included in the layer attribute table.

The District boundary definitions are primarily based on the California county boundaries. The Board of
Equalization (BOE) County and City Boundary web service is the authoritative source of County boundaries
and this was used to digitize the district boundaries based on the data from March 2023. This data is
solely for informational purposes. District 9 has been operating independent of the Central Region since
November 1, 2015. Kern County remains the only split county in the state, between Districts 6 and 9
respectively. The BOE data was also used to create the district boundary layers along the coastline.
[Data source](https://gisdata-caltrans.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/0144574f750f4ccc88749004aca6eb0c_0/about)

DISTRICT.name: DISTRICT
DISTRICT.description: The territorial division (as for administrative or electoral purposes). Values are 1-12.

Region.name: Region
Region.description: The administrative area of the district.

source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo_re.name: shn_lines
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo_re.source_description: Geographic tables to be used in creating maps and other GIS-related visualizations.
source.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.geo_re.description:
StateHighways are state highways based upon the Caltrans Linear Referencing System. Each record in this
layer represents a highway segment where the county, route, postmile prefix, and postmile suffix are the
same. Each segment has a begin and end postmile. This geometry is based upon the extraction from TSN on
10 October 2022.

Each segment is coded with the district, county, route, postmile prefix (if any), begin postmile, end
postmile, and postmile suffix (if any). One additional field - AlignCode - should be understood by the
user for effective use of this data set (refer to the Entity and Attribute Information section of this
metadata).

AlignCode indicates if the state highway segment is on a Right alignment, Left alignment, Right side of
an independent alignment or Left side of an independent alignment. Where TSN (Transportation System
Network - the source database) considers most state highways to have just a centerline, the underlying
LRS (Linear Referencing System) linework that these segments are based upon has a line for each direction.

On undivided highways the right and left lines are identical. On divided highways the right and left are
separated, but TSN treats the segment as a single centerline. In the last case where highways are not only
divided but also have different lengths (where the right and left carriageway diverge around physical
obstacles or are separated onto different one-way streets) the underlying linework has dual carriageways,
and TSN treats the segment as an "independent alignment".  In some cases (especially for small-scale
mapping), it may be appropriate to display linework for just one alignment (e.g. Right alignment), and
only for the other side where TSN indicates independent right and left alignments. In other cases (e.g.
large-scale mapping), it may be appropriate to display postmiles for both alignments, regardless of how
TSN treats the segment.

The bOdometer and eOdometer fields represent the actual distance in miles from the start of the highway
to the beginning and end of each highway section.  This is in contrast to the begin and end postmile
values, which no longer represent these values as each highway is realigned (and made longer or shorter)
over time. [Data
source](https://gisdata-caltrans.opendata.arcgis.com/datasets/77f2d7ba94e040a78bfbe36feb6279da_0/about)

District.name: District
District.description: The district in which the highway is located. Values are 1-12.

bOdometer.name: bOdometer
bOdometer.description:
Represents the distance in miles from the start of the highway to
the beginning and end of each highway section.

eOdometer.name: eOdometer
eOdometer.description:
Represents the distance in miles from the start of the highway to
the beginning and end of each highway section.

RouteType.name: RouteType
RouteType.description: The type of route of the highway. Values are State, US, and Interstate.

Direction.name: Direction
Direction.description:
The direction of the highway.
Values are NB, SB, EB, and WB (north-, south-, east-, and west-bound).

Shape__Length.name: Shape__Length
Shape__Length.description: The length of the state highway.

geometry.name: geometry
geometry.description: The geometry of the state highway.

macro.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.get_sno.name: get_snowflake_refresh_warehouse
macro.caldata_mdsa_caltrans_pems.get_sno.description:
Full refreshes of incremental models sometimes need a little bit of
extra firepower. This returns an appropriate (i.e. 4XL) warehouse
for that case. Use it with caution! This returns large if either of
the following conditions are met:

* The target table doesn't exist (i.e., hasn't been built yet)
* The `--full-refresh` flag is set via the CLI.

arguments.name: small
arguments.description: Snowflake warehouse size to use when *not* performing a full-refresh

arguments.name: big
arguments.description: Snowflake warehouse size to use when performing a full-refresh
